title: 'If they catch me, it's over for me': Senegal's LGBTQ+ community lives in daily fear of repression
subhead: In March, Senegal passed a law increasing penalties for same-sex relations, leaving LGBTQ+ people living in fear. Several described their deeply uncertain daily lives, gripped by the constant anxiety of being arrested.

Iran declares Hormuz strait closed, US military insists traffic flowing
subhead: Iran attacked Gulf countries, including Qatar, the United Arab Emirates and Oman, after US strikes in retaliation for an attack against a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: 'I would do a line before service, hidden in the bathroom': How cocaine is flooding the restaurant industry
subhead: Cocaine at work (2/3). From fast-food joints to Michelin-starred restaurants, drug use has become as commonplace as it is alarming in an industry driven by the pursuit of performance, a toxic party culture and grueling working conditions.

title: French women's baseballers 'not going to give up' after national team is shut down
subhead: The French national women's baseball team, crowned champions of Europe twice since 2019, has been suspended by the French Federation of Baseball and Softball.

title: Simon Abkarian, star of 'De Gaulle': 'At school in Lebanon, my nickname was de Gaulle'
subhead: The actor and theater director plays Charles de Gaulle in the recently released two-part film. In this article, he explains how he prepared for the role of France's World War II hero.

Martine Barrat, a photographer without fear or limits
subhead: At 93, the artist has lost none of her vitality or curiosity. She lives at the legendary Hotel Chelsea in New York, where she remains one of the last year-round artist residents. Today, her work is the subject of a retrospective at the Rencontres d'Arles photography festival.

title: Silicon Valley's next generation feels the AI blues
subhead: In California, the rise of artificial intelligence no longer generates the unanimous enthusiasm it once did. Anxiety is mounting, especially among students and engineers, as jobs have been cut and democratic risks have grown more threatening.

title: Is air conditioning good or bad? Join this (fake) group chat for (real) analysis
subhead: Each time a new heatwave hits, the question of air conditioning and its environmental impacts crops up in the public debate. Here are the key points to help you understand what is at stake.
